description: Prisma schema validates and generates client without errors
steps: Run npx prisma generate
Verify no errors in output
Verify src/generated/prisma/ directory is created

description: Database migrations run successfully against PostgreSQL
steps: Run npx prisma db push
Verify all tables are created in PostgreSQL
Verify elections, candidates, issue_categories, raw_materials, candidate_positions, question_sets, questions, user_sessions, user_answers, users tables exist

description: Seed script populates database with sample election data
steps: Run seed script
Verify at least one election is created
Verify at least two candidates per election
Verify issue categories are created
Verify candidate positions are populated

description: Unique constraint on candidate_positions(candidate_id, issue_id) prevents duplicates
steps: Attempt to insert two positions for same candidate+issue
Verify second insert fails with unique constraint error

description: GET /api/elections returns list of elections
steps: Send GET request to /api/elections
Verify 200 status
Verify response is JSON array of election objects
Verify each election has id, name, type, district, electionDate, status

description: GET /api/elections?zip={zipCode} filters elections by zip code
steps: Send GET request with valid zip code
Verify only elections matching that district are returned
Send GET request with nonexistent zip code
Verify empty array is returned

description: GET /api/elections/{electionId}/candidates returns candidates for an election
steps: Send GET request with valid election ID
Verify 200 status
Verify response contains candidate objects with id, name, party, incumbent
Send GET request with invalid election ID
Verify 404 status

description: GET /api/elections/{electionId}/questions returns question set
steps: Send GET request with valid election ID
Verify 200 status
Verify response contains questions array
Verify each question has questionText, positiveDirection, background, displayOrder
Verify questions are sorted by displayOrder

description: POST /api/match accepts answers and returns match results
steps: Send POST with election_id and answers array
Verify 200 status
Verify response contains candidates array with matchPercentage
Verify matchPercentage is between 0 and 100
Verify response contains issueBreakdown per candidate

description: POST /api/match handles 'not_interested' answers correctly
steps: Send POST with some answers as 'not_interested'
Verify those issues are excluded from match calculation
Verify matchPercentage is calculated only on answered issues

description: POST /api/match creates anonymous user session
steps: Send POST /api/match with valid data
Verify a user_session record is created in DB
Verify user_answers records are created
Verify no PII is stored in the session

description: GET /api/candidates/{candidateId}/positions returns detailed positions
steps: Send GET request with valid candidate ID
Verify 200 status
Verify response contains positions array
Verify each position has positionSummary, positionScore, confidence, source
Verify supporting evidence and source links are included

description: API returns proper error responses for invalid inputs
steps: Send POST /api/match with empty body
Verify 400 status with error message
Send POST /api/match with invalid election_id
Verify 404 status
Send POST /api/match with malformed answers
Verify 400 status

description: API endpoints handle missing candidate positions gracefully
steps: Create candidate with no positions
Request match results
Verify candidate appears in results with 0% or indication of no data
Verify no server error occurs

description: Match engine calculates correct percentage for identical positions
steps: Provide user answers identical to candidate positions
Verify matchPercentage is 100.0

description: Match engine calculates correct percentage for opposite positions
steps: Provide user answers that are exact opposites of candidate positions
Verify matchPercentage is 0.0

description: Match engine applies stronger weight to strongly_agree/strongly_disagree
steps: Create scenario where strong stance on one issue vs weak stance on another
Verify the strongly-held issue has more influence on final score
Compare with equal-weight scenario to confirm difference

description: Match engine excludes 'not_interested' issues from calculation
steps: Calculate match with all issues answered
Calculate match with some issues as not_interested
Verify not_interested issues don't affect the final percentage

description: Match engine handles edge case of all issues 'not_interested'
steps: Provide all answers as not_interested (empty userAnswers)
Verify matchPercentage returns 0 without error

description: Match engine handles candidate with no position on some issues
steps: Provide user answers for issues where candidate has no position
Verify those issues are skipped
Verify calculation only uses issues both parties have positions on

description: Match engine correctly ranks multiple candidates
steps: Calculate match for 3+ candidates
Verify candidates are ranked by matchPercentage descending
Verify ranking reflects actual position alignment

description: Match engine returns issue-by-issue breakdown
steps: Calculate match result
Verify issueBreakdown array contains entry for each compared issue
Verify each entry has userScore, candidateScore, similarity, weight

description: Landing page loads and displays VoteMatch branding
steps: Navigate to /
Verify 'VoteMatch' heading is visible
Verify tagline text is visible
Verify 'Get Started' button is visible

description: Landing page 'Get Started' button navigates to quiz flow
steps: Click 'Get Started' button
Verify navigation to /quiz or district selection page

description: Landing page is responsive on mobile viewport
steps: Set viewport to 375px width
Verify all content is visible without horizontal scroll
Verify text is readable
Verify button is tappable size (min 44x44px)

description: District selection page allows entering zip code
steps: Navigate to district selection page
Verify zip code input field is present
Enter a valid zip code
Submit the form

description: District selection shows available elections after zip code entry
steps: Enter valid zip code
Verify elections list appears
Verify each election shows name and date
Verify user can select an election

description: District selection shows candidates after election selection
steps: Select an election
Verify candidates list appears
Verify each candidate shows name and party
Verify proceed/continue button appears

description: District selection handles no elections found gracefully
steps: Enter zip code with no matching elections
Verify friendly message is shown (not error)
Verify user can try a different zip code

description: District selection validates zip code format
steps: Enter invalid zip code (letters, too short, too long)
Verify validation error message appears
Verify form does not submit

description: Quiz page displays questions one at a time or in a list
steps: Navigate to quiz page for a valid election
Verify at least one question is displayed
Verify question text is visible and readable

description: Quiz page shows 6 answer options per question
steps: View a quiz question
Verify 6 options are shown: Strongly Agree, Agree, Neutral, Disagree, Strongly Disagree, Not Interested
Verify options are clearly labeled

description: Quiz page allows selecting one answer per question
steps: Click on an answer option
Verify it is visually selected
Click a different option for the same question
Verify the first is deselected and the new one is selected

description: Quiz page has expandable background explanation per question
steps: View a quiz question
Verify background expand trigger is visible
Click to expand background
Verify background text appears
Click to collapse
Verify background text is hidden

description: Quiz page shows progress indicator
steps: Start the quiz
Verify progress indicator shows current question / total questions
Answer a question and advance
Verify progress indicator updates

description: Quiz page submit button sends answers to match API
steps: Answer all questions
Click submit/see results button
Verify POST request is sent to /api/match
Verify request contains election_id and answers array

description: Quiz page handles partial completion (not all questions answered)
steps: Answer only some questions
Click submit
Verify unanswered questions are either prompted or treated as not_interested

description: Quiz page is responsive on mobile viewport
steps: Set viewport to 375px width
Verify questions and options are fully visible
Verify options are tappable without overlap
Verify no horizontal scroll

description: Quiz page uses plain language at 6th-grade reading level
steps: Read each question text
Verify no political jargon or technical terms
Verify sentences are short and clear
Verify one concept per question

description: Results page shows match ranking with percentages
steps: Complete quiz and view results
Verify each candidate is listed with name and match percentage
Verify candidates are ordered by match percentage descending
Verify wording says 'policy positions are closest to yours' not 'vote for'

description: Results page shows issue-by-issue comparison
steps: View results page
Navigate to or expand issue-by-issue comparison
Verify each issue shows user's choice vs each candidate's position
Verify match/mismatch is visually indicated

description: Results page shows source attribution for candidate positions
steps: View candidate position in results
Verify source label is shown (official website / voting record / candidate self-report)
Verify source link is clickable and opens in new tab

description: Results page shows 'No public position found' when applicable
steps: View results for candidate with missing positions
Verify 'No public position found' label appears for that issue
Verify it is clearly distinguished from actual positions

description: Results page is responsive on mobile viewport
steps: Set viewport to 375px width
Verify match ranking is readable
Verify issue comparison is navigable
Verify no horizontal scroll

description: Results page handles candidate with conflicting self-report vs public record
steps: View results for candidate with both ai_extracted and candidate_self_report positions
Verify both are displayed
Verify they are clearly labeled and distinguished

description: Results page allows navigating back to retake quiz
steps: View results page
Find 'Retake Quiz' or back navigation
Click it
Verify user returns to quiz with answers cleared

description: Full user flow works end-to-end: landing → district → quiz → results
steps: Start at landing page
Click Get Started
Enter zip code and select election
Answer all quiz questions
Submit and view results
Verify match percentages are displayed correctly

description: Browser back button works correctly through the flow
steps: Complete the quiz flow to results
Press browser back button
Verify return to quiz page with answers preserved
Press back again
Verify return to district selection

description: Direct URL navigation to /quiz without election context redirects
steps: Navigate directly to /quiz without prior district selection
Verify redirect to district selection or helpful error message

description: User sessions are anonymous — no PII stored
steps: Complete a quiz
Check user_sessions table
Verify no IP address, device info, or cookies linking to identity
Verify session only has election_id and created_at

description: No user registration required for core voter flow
steps: Navigate through entire flow: landing → district → quiz → results
Verify no login/signup prompt appears
Verify no authentication headers required in API calls

description: Quiz page loads within 3 seconds on simulated 3G connection
steps: Throttle network to slow 3G
Navigate to quiz page
Verify page is interactive within 3 seconds

description: Match calculation completes within 500ms for 15 questions × 5 candidates
steps: Set up 5 candidates with 15 issue positions each
Submit 15 answers via /api/match
Verify response time is under 500ms

description: Question wording contains no leading language
steps: Review all generated questions
Verify no emotionally charged words
Verify no implied correct answer
Verify balanced framing

description: Match algorithm gives no advantage to any candidate regardless of data source
steps: Compare match calculation for ai_extracted vs candidate_self_report positions
Verify identical treatment in scoring
Verify no bonus or penalty for claimed profiles

description: Seed data covers a realistic election scenario
steps: Run seed script
Verify at least 1 election with 3+ candidates
Verify at least 10 issue categories
Verify positions cover high/medium/low confidence
Verify questions are pre-generated for the election

description: Seed data includes candidates with missing positions for edge case testing
steps: Verify at least one candidate has positions on only some issues
Verify at least one issue has 'position unclear' or low confidence
Verify results display handles these gracefully

description: Quiz page is keyboard-navigable
steps: Tab through quiz page
Verify all answer options are focusable
Verify Enter/Space selects an answer
Verify submit button is reachable via Tab

description: All interactive elements have proper aria labels
steps: Inspect answer option elements
Verify radio inputs or buttons have aria-label or associated label
Verify progress indicator has aria-valuenow/aria-valuemax

description: Color contrast meets WCAG AA standards
steps: Check text contrast ratios on landing, quiz, and results pages
Verify body text has at least 4.5:1 contrast ratio
Verify headings have at least 3:1 contrast ratio

description: API errors return structured JSON error responses
steps: Send malformed request to each API endpoint
Verify all return JSON with error field
Verify appropriate HTTP status codes (400, 404, 500)

description: UI shows user-friendly error when API fails
steps: Simulate API failure (stop server or mock error)
Attempt quiz submission
Verify user-friendly error message appears (not raw error)
Verify user can retry

description: UI handles network timeout gracefully
steps: Simulate slow network
Submit quiz
Verify loading indicator appears
Simulate timeout
Verify timeout message and retry option

description: Project builds without errors (npm run build)
steps: Run npm run build
Verify exit code 0
Verify no TypeScript errors
Verify .next build output is generated

description: ESLint passes without errors
steps: Run npm run lint
Verify exit code 0
Verify no lint errors

description: Dev server starts and serves pages
steps: Run npm run dev
Verify server starts on port 3000
Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Verify landing page loads

description: UI text is externalized and ready for multi-language support
steps: Verify UI strings are not hardcoded in components
Verify a translation mechanism is in place or planned
Verify language can be switched without code changes

description: Landing page has proper meta tags for SEO
steps: View page source of landing page
Verify <title> contains 'VoteMatch'
Verify <meta name='description'> is present and descriptive
Verify SSR renders the content (not client-only)

description: Results page is server-rendered for shareability
steps: View page source of a results page
Verify candidate match data is in the HTML (not loaded client-side only)

description: Collector LLM prompt searches candidate official campaign website for policy info
steps: Call collector with a candidate name, office, and district
Verify output includes search results from official campaign website
Verify output includes /issues, /platform, /about page content if found
Verify 'not found' is explicitly marked if no official website exists

description: Collector searches voting record databases for incumbent candidates
steps: Call collector with an incumbent candidate
Verify output includes voting record search results
Verify source_type is 'voting_record'
Verify raw_text contains relevant vote descriptions

description: Collector searches local news coverage for candidate policy mentions
steps: Call collector with candidate name and district
Verify output includes local news search results
Verify source_type is 'news'
Verify raw_text contains policy-relevant excerpts

description: Collector searches public social media posts for policy statements
steps: Call collector with candidate name
Verify output includes social media search results
Verify source_type is 'social_media'
Verify only policy-relevant posts are included

description: Collector outputs structured RawMaterial records with required fields
steps: Run collector for a candidate
Verify each result has source_url, source_type, raw_text, access_date
Verify no stance judgments are made in raw_text (pure collection)
Verify results are stored in RawMaterial table

description: Collector explicitly marks 'not found' for source types with no results
steps: Run collector for a candidate with limited public presence
Verify each source type (official_website, voting_record, news, social_media) is attempted
Verify source types with no results are marked as 'not found' in output

description: URL verifier checks HTTP status for each source_url
steps: Run URL verification on collected RawMaterial records
Verify each source_url is visited programmatically
Verify HTTP 200 responses set url_verified = true
Verify non-200 responses set url_verified = false

description: URL verifier confirms raw_text exists on the linked page
steps: Run content verification on URL-verified RawMaterial records
Verify page content is fetched and compared to raw_text
Verify matching content sets content_verified = true
Verify non-matching content sets content_verified = false

description: Failed URL verifications are flagged for review or discarded
steps: Create RawMaterial with invalid source_url
Run verification pipeline
Verify record is flagged with url_verified = false
Verify flagged records are excluded from analyzer input

description: Analyzer extracts structured positions from verified raw materials
steps: Provide verified RawMaterial records for a candidate
Run analyzer
Verify output contains CandidatePosition records
Verify each position has issue_id, position_summary, position_score

description: Analyzer assigns position_score on -2 to +2 scale correctly
steps: Run analyzer on raw materials with clear pro/con stances
Verify strongly supportive positions get score near +2
Verify strongly opposing positions get score near -2
Verify neutral/ambiguous positions get score near 0

description: Analyzer assigns confidence levels (high/medium/low) to each position
steps: Run analyzer on raw materials with varying evidence quality
Verify positions backed by voting records get 'high' confidence
Verify positions from single news mention get 'medium' or 'low'
Verify positions with no direct evidence get 'low'

description: Analyzer marks low-confidence positions as 'Position unclear'
steps: Run analyzer on ambiguous raw materials
Verify low-confidence positions have notes containing 'Position unclear'
Verify these are not force-classified into a strong stance

description: Analyzer identifies 'said a lot but no substantive position' cases
steps: Provide raw materials where candidate discusses an issue without taking a stance
Run analyzer
Verify output notes indicate no substantive position found
Verify position is not fabricated from vague language

description: Analyzer detects contradictory statements and preserves both
steps: Provide raw materials with contradictory positions on same issue
Run analyzer
Verify notes field describes the contradiction
Verify supporting_evidence includes quotes from both stances

description: Analyzer includes supporting_evidence with raw_material_id and relevant_quote
steps: Run analyzer on raw materials
Verify each CandidatePosition has supporting_evidence array
Verify each evidence item has raw_material_id referencing source
Verify relevant_quote contains the actual text supporting the judgment

description: Analyzer maps positions to predefined IssueCategory framework
steps: Define issue categories for the election level (state/local/federal)
Run analyzer
Verify each position references a valid issue_id from IssueCategory table
Verify no positions reference non-existent issue categories

description: End-to-end pipeline runs: candidate → collector → verification → analyzer → positions
steps: Provide a candidate name, office, and district
Run full pipeline
Verify RawMaterial records are created
Verify URL verification runs on all records
Verify CandidatePosition records are created from verified materials

description: Pipeline is idempotent — re-running does not create duplicates
steps: Run pipeline for a candidate
Run pipeline again for the same candidate
Verify no duplicate RawMaterial records
Verify CandidatePosition records are updated, not duplicated

description: Pipeline supports incremental updates at key moments
steps: Run initial pipeline for a candidate
Simulate new public statement (add new source)
Run incremental update
Verify only new sources are collected and analyzed
Verify existing positions are updated if new evidence changes them

description: Pipeline runs for all candidates in an election as a batch
steps: Configure pipeline for a target election
Run batch pipeline
Verify collector runs for each candidate
Verify analyzer runs for each candidate
Verify all candidates have CandidatePosition records

description: Question generator calculates divergence score per issue across candidates
steps: Fetch all CandidatePosition data for an election
Calculate divergence = max(position_scores) - min(position_scores) per issue
Verify divergence scores are correct for each issue
Verify issues with identical positions get divergence = 0

description: Question generator selects top 8-15 most divergent issues
steps: Calculate divergence scores for all issues
Verify issues are sorted by divergence descending
Verify 8-15 issues are selected
Verify low-divergence issues are excluded

description: LLM generates user-facing question for each selected issue
steps: Provide selected issues and candidate position summaries
Call LLM question generation prompt
Verify output contains question_text, positive_direction, background per issue
Verify reading_level_check passes

description: Generated questions use everyday language at 6th-grade reading level
steps: Review all generated questions
Verify no political jargon or technical terms
Verify short sentences with simple vocabulary
Verify one concept per question

description: Each generated question has a clear positive_direction defined
steps: Review generated question set
Verify every question has positive_direction field
Verify positive_direction clearly states what 'agree' means
Verify score mapping is consistent with positive_direction

description: Each generated question has a plain-language background explainer
steps: Review generated question set
Verify every question has background field with 2-3 sentences
Verify background uses plain language (no jargon)
Verify background provides relevant context for the issue

description: Generated questions are stored as QuestionSet linked to election
steps: Generate questions for an election
Verify QuestionSet record created with election_id and generated_at
Verify Question records created with question_set_id
Verify questions have display_order assigned

description: QuestionSet is regenerated when candidate position data changes
steps: Generate initial QuestionSet for an election
Update a candidate's position data
Trigger regeneration
Verify new QuestionSet is created
Verify questions reflect updated divergence

description: Question count is between 8 and 15
steps: Generate questions for election with many issues (>15)
Verify no more than 15 questions generated
Generate questions for election with few divergent issues
Verify at least 8 questions generated (or max available if <8 issues)

description: LLM generates natural language match explanation per candidate
steps: Complete match calculation
Call LLM explanation generator with user answers, candidate positions, match scores
Verify output contains a brief match summary per candidate
Verify explanation references specific issues where user and candidate agree/disagree

description: Match explanations use neutral, non-directive language
steps: Generate match explanations
Verify no language suggesting who to vote for
Verify wording focuses on policy alignment, not endorsement
Verify tone is informational, not persuasive

description: Match explanations can be pre-generated and cached
steps: Pre-generate common explanation templates
Verify cached explanations are used when available
Verify cache invalidation when positions change
Verify fallback to live LLM generation when cache miss

description: NextAuth.js configured for candidate authentication
steps: Verify NextAuth.js is installed and configured
Verify auth API routes exist at /api/auth/*
Verify session provider wraps candidate portal pages
Verify JWT or session-based auth is functional

description: Candidate registration with email and password
steps: Navigate to candidate registration page
Enter email and password
Submit registration form
Verify user record created in users table with role='candidate'
Verify password is hashed (not stored in plaintext)

description: Email verification required before profile claim
steps: Register a new candidate account
Verify email verification is sent
Attempt to claim profile without verifying email
Verify claim is rejected
Verify email, then retry claim
Verify claim proceeds

description: Candidate login and logout flow works correctly
steps: Navigate to candidate login page
Enter valid credentials
Verify redirect to candidate dashboard
Click logout
Verify redirect to login page
Verify protected routes are inaccessible after logout

description: Candidate portal routes are protected — require authentication
steps: Navigate to /candidate/dashboard without auth
Verify redirect to login page
Navigate to /api/candidates/me/positions without auth
Verify 401 response

description: Claim Your Profile page accessible from candidate results view
steps: View results page showing a candidate
Verify 'Claim Your Profile' link is visible for unclaimed candidates
Click the link
Verify navigation to claim flow page

description: POST /api/candidates/claim endpoint accepts claim request
steps: Send POST with candidate_id and verification_info
Verify 200 status for valid request
Verify claim record is created
Verify 400 for missing fields
Verify 404 for invalid candidate_id

description: Claims enter manual review queue in MVP
steps: Submit a profile claim
Verify claim status is 'pending_review'
Verify candidate.claimed remains false until approved
Verify admin can approve/reject claims

description: Approved claim marks candidate as claimed and links to user
steps: Submit and approve a profile claim
Verify candidate.claimed = true
Verify candidate.claimed_by = user.id
Verify candidate dashboard shows claimed profile

description: Already-claimed candidates cannot be claimed by another user
steps: Claim and approve a candidate profile
Attempt to claim same candidate with different user
Verify claim is rejected with appropriate message

description: GET /api/candidates/me/positions returns own position data
steps: Authenticate as a candidate who has claimed a profile
Send GET to /api/candidates/me/positions
Verify 200 status with positions array
Verify positions include both ai_extracted and candidate_self_report entries

description: Candidate dashboard displays own positions with source labels
steps: Log in as claimed candidate
Navigate to candidate dashboard
Verify all positions are listed with issue name
Verify each position shows source (AI Extracted / Self-Report)
Verify confidence level is displayed

description: PUT /api/candidates/me/positions/{positionId} updates a position
steps: Authenticate as claimed candidate
Send PUT with updated position_summary and position_score
Verify 200 status
Verify position is updated in database
Verify source is set to 'candidate_self_report'

description: Candidate can add new position for uncovered issue
steps: Authenticate as claimed candidate with missing positions on some issues
Add position for an uncovered issue
Verify new CandidatePosition record created
Verify source is 'candidate_self_report'
Verify position appears in results for that candidate

description: Self-report contradicting AI-extracted shows both positions to voters
steps: Have candidate self-report a position that contradicts AI-extracted position
View results as a voter
Verify both positions are displayed for that issue
Verify labels clearly distinguish 'Public Record' from 'Candidate Self-Report'

description: Position changes by candidate trigger question set regeneration
steps: Candidate updates a position
Verify divergence scores are recalculated
Verify QuestionSet is regenerated if divergence changes significantly
Verify updated questions reflect new position landscape

description: GET /api/candidates/me/insights returns anonymized voter data
steps: Authenticate as claimed candidate
Ensure ≥50 user sessions exist for the election
Send GET to /api/candidates/me/insights
Verify 200 status with aggregated data
Verify no individual user data is identifiable

description: Insights require minimum 50 user sessions for privacy
steps: Authenticate as claimed candidate
Ensure <50 user sessions exist
Send GET to /api/candidates/me/insights
Verify response indicates insufficient data
Add sessions to reach 50, retry
Verify data is now returned

description: Insights include voter issue priority ranking
steps: Generate insights with sufficient sessions
Verify response includes issue ranking by voter interest
Verify ranking reflects which issues voters answered (vs skipped)
Verify strong stances are weighted higher

description: Insights include candidate match analysis vs district voters
steps: Generate insights
Verify response includes candidate's average match percentage
Verify breakdown by issue shows where candidate aligns/diverges from voters
Verify comparison against other candidates

description: Insights include competitive gap analysis
steps: Generate insights for election with multiple candidates
Verify response shows which issues competitors outperform on
Verify data is useful for campaign strategy adjustment
Verify all data is aggregated and anonymized

description: Aggregated data dashboard shows election-level voter analytics
steps: Navigate to consulting dashboard
Verify dashboard shows total sessions, completion rate, avg match scores
Verify data is filterable by election
Verify all displayed data is anonymized

description: LLM generates deep analysis report from aggregated voter data
steps: Select an election with sufficient data
Trigger report generation
Verify LLM receives only aggregated data (no PII)
Verify report includes issue priority analysis and strategy recommendations
Verify report is downloadable

description: Consultant workbench supports serving multiple clients
steps: Log in as consultant
Verify ability to view data for multiple elections
Verify reports can be generated per candidate
Verify consultant can add professional notes to reports

description: Campaign strategy recommendations generated from voter alignment data
steps: Generate strategy report for a candidate
Verify recommendations reference specific issues where candidate underperforms
Verify suggestions are actionable and specific
Verify no individual voter data is exposed

description: Next.js frontend deploys to Vercel successfully
steps: Configure Vercel project for VoteMatch
Push code to trigger deployment
Verify deployment succeeds
Verify landing page loads on production URL

description: PostgreSQL database provisioned on cloud provider
steps: Provision PostgreSQL on Railway, Supabase, or equivalent
Run prisma db push against production database
Verify all tables are created
Verify seed data can be loaded

description: Redis cache configured for production
steps: Provision Redis on Upstash or equivalent
Configure REDIS_URL environment variable
Verify cache read/write operations work
Verify question sets and match results can be cached

description: Production environment variables configured securely
steps: Set DATABASE_URL in Vercel environment
Set REDIS_URL in Vercel environment
Set NEXTAUTH_SECRET for auth
Verify .env is gitignored and not deployed
Verify app functions correctly with production env vars

description: Custom domain configured with HTTPS
steps: Configure custom domain in Vercel
Verify DNS propagation
Verify HTTPS certificate is provisioned
Verify site loads on custom domain with HTTPS

description: GitHub Actions or cron job triggers pipeline runs on schedule
steps: Create GitHub Actions workflow for data pipeline
Configure schedule (pre-election full run, periodic incremental)
Verify pipeline runs successfully on trigger
Verify pipeline results are stored in production database

description: Production build and deployment pipeline with CI/CD
steps: Push code to main branch
Verify CI runs lint, type-check, and tests
Verify deployment triggers on CI success
Verify failed CI blocks deployment

description: Candidate insights API returns only aggregated data — no individual sessions
steps: Request insights for an election with 100+ sessions
Verify response contains aggregate metrics only
Verify no individual session IDs, answers, or timestamps exposed
Verify response cannot be reverse-engineered to individual users

description: Minimum 50 user sessions enforced before aggregation is available
steps: Request insights with 49 sessions
Verify request is rejected with insufficient data message
Add one more session (total 50)
Verify insights become available

description: No user PII is sent in any LLM API calls
steps: Audit all LLM API call points in codebase
Verify no user IP, session ID, or personal data in prompts
Verify only aggregated or candidate data is sent to LLM
Verify logging does not capture PII alongside LLM calls

description: Candidate authentication uses secure password hashing
steps: Register a candidate account
Inspect users table in database
Verify password_hash is stored (not plaintext password)
Verify bcrypt or equivalent secure hashing is used

description: API rate limiting prevents abuse on public endpoints
steps: Send 100 rapid requests to /api/elections
Verify rate limiting kicks in after threshold
Verify 429 Too Many Requests response
Verify legitimate requests still work after cooldown

description: HTTPS enforced in production — HTTP redirects to HTTPS
steps: Access production site via HTTP
Verify redirect to HTTPS
Verify all API endpoints require HTTPS
Verify no mixed content warnings

description: Quiz completion rate is tracked and reportable
steps: Complete several quizzes (some partial, some full)
Query completion rate metric
Verify rate = (completed sessions / total sessions) * 100
Verify target is >70% per PRD §9

description: Pipeline output quality can be manually verified
steps: Run pipeline for a candidate
View raw materials and extracted positions
Verify audit trail from source_url → raw_text → position_summary
Verify quality can be spot-checked by human reviewer

description: Data accuracy monitoring compares pipeline output to manual review
steps: Run pipeline for a candidate with known positions
Compare pipeline-extracted positions to manual ground truth
Calculate accuracy metric (correct positions / total positions)
Verify accuracy reporting is available

description: Candidate data coverage is tracked per election
steps: Query coverage for a target election
Verify coverage = (candidates with positions / total candidates) * 100
Verify target is 100% for target district per PRD §9

description: Fetch candidate lists from state election commission APIs/websites
steps: Configure target state election commission data source
Run candidate list fetcher for a target district
Verify Election records are created with name, type, district, state, election_date, status
Verify Candidate records are created with name, party, incumbent, official_website

description: Fetch candidate data from FEC public data for federal elections
steps: Query FEC public data API for a target election
Verify federal candidate records are imported
Verify party and incumbent status are populated
Verify data matches FEC source

description: Fetch candidate data from third-party aggregators (BallotReady, Vote Smart)
steps: Query BallotReady or Vote Smart API for a target district
Verify candidate records are imported and deduplicated
Verify data supplements state/FEC sources
Verify official_website is populated when available

description: Candidate list acquisition deduplicates across multiple data sources
steps: Import candidates from state commission and FEC
Verify same candidate from different sources is not duplicated
Verify merged record has the most complete data
Verify election-candidate relationships are correct

description: District selection supports address input in addition to zip code
steps: Navigate to district selection page
Verify both address and zip code inputs are available
Enter a street address
Verify system resolves address to correct district and elections

description: Geocoding API maps address/zip to legislative district
steps: Send address to geocoding service
Verify correct legislative district is returned
Verify works for federal, state, and local districts
Verify fallback to zip-based lookup when geocoding fails

description: LLM dynamic translation for content beyond static UI strings
steps: View a quiz question in non-default language
Verify question_text, background, and position_summary are translated by LLM
Verify translation quality is natural and accurate
Verify translated content is cached to avoid redundant LLM calls

description: Redis caches pre-generated question sets to avoid redundant DB queries
steps: Request questions for an election
Verify questions are fetched from DB and stored in Redis
Request same questions again
Verify second request is served from Redis cache, not DB
Verify cache is invalidated when QuestionSet is regenerated

description: Redis caches match results for shareable result URLs
steps: Complete a quiz and generate match results
Verify results are cached in Redis by session ID
Access results URL again
Verify cached results are returned without recalculation
Verify cache TTL is appropriate

description: Issue framework varies by election level (federal vs state vs local)
steps: Define federal-level issues (immigration, defense, foreign policy)
Define state-level issues (education, healthcare, taxation)
Define local-level issues (zoning, school districts, public transit)
Verify IssueCategory.level field correctly filters issues per election type
Verify analyzer uses level-appropriate framework

description: Admin can define and manage issue categories per election level
steps: Access admin interface or CLI tool for issue management
Create new issue category with name, level, display_name_en, display_name_zh
Edit existing issue category
Verify changes are reflected in question generation and analyzer

description: Admin can manually create elections and enter candidate data
steps: Access admin interface or CLI
Create a new Election record with all required fields
Add Candidate records linked to the election
Verify data appears in voter-facing API endpoints

description: Admin can approve or reject candidate profile claims
steps: View pending claim requests in admin interface
Approve a claim — verify candidate.claimed = true
Reject a claim — verify candidate.claimed remains false
Verify claimant is notified of decision

description: Pre-election full scrape cycle runs for all candidates in target election
steps: Configure target election for full scrape
Run full scrape — collector + verification + analyzer for ALL candidates
Verify all candidates have fresh RawMaterial and CandidatePosition records
Verify existing stale data is updated, not duplicated
Verify full scrape is distinct from incremental updates

description: Full audit trail from source URL to displayed voter position is traceable
steps: View a candidate position in results page
Verify source attribution links to original source_url
Trace backward: position → supporting_evidence → raw_material → source_url
Verify every link in the chain is intact and verifiable
Verify access_date shows when data was collected

description: Position data source labels match the three-tier hierarchy from PRD
steps: View AI-extracted position — verify labeled as source type + original link
View candidate self-reported position — verify labeled 'Candidate Self-Report'
View missing position — verify labeled 'No public position found'
Verify all three tiers are visually distinct in the UI

description: User satisfaction feedback collection mechanism exists
steps: Complete quiz and view results
Verify satisfaction prompt or feedback mechanism appears
Submit satisfaction rating (1-5 scale)
Verify rating is stored anonymously
Verify aggregated satisfaction can be queried (target >4/5 per PRD §9)

description: District voter coverage is tracked as percentage of eligible voters
steps: Query unique voter sessions per district
Compare against estimated eligible voters in district
Verify coverage = (unique sessions / eligible voters) * 100
Verify target is >1% per PRD §9

description: White-label embeddable quiz widget for media and civic organizations
steps: Generate embed code for a specific election
Embed widget in a third-party HTML page
Verify quiz flow works within embedded context
Verify branding is customizable (white-label)
Verify results data flows back to VoteMatch analytics

description: Embedded widget supports custom styling and domain whitelisting
steps: Configure custom CSS/theme for embedded widget
Set allowed domains for embed
Verify widget renders with custom styling
Verify widget rejects loading from non-whitelisted domains
